summary:
The World Health Organization defines profoundly deaf individuals based on an auditory threshold greater than or equal to 90 decibels across all frequencies tested during a standard audiological evaluation. This classification reflects severe impairment where even amplified sounds may not be heard without assistive devices like cochlear implants or advanced hearing aids designed specifically for those at these extreme levels of hearing loss severity.
 To answer your specific query about classifying profound hearing loss using only two sentences:

The WHO categorizes profound hearing loss when there are auditory thresholds above 90 decibels; it signifies extremely poor hearing sensitivity requiring specialized interventions beyond conventional amplification methods. Sentence count: 2
